tangerine-and-prosecco-sorbet.name: Tangerine And Prosecco Sorbet
instructions: Combine sugar and 3/4 cup water in small saucepan. Stir over medium heat until sugar dissolves. Increase heat and bring to boil. Transfer syrup to medium bowl and chill until cold, about 2 hours.
Add strained tangerine juice, Prosecco, and finely grated tangerine peel to syrup; whisk to blend well. Transfer mixture to ice cream maker and process according to manufacturer's instructions.
Transfer sorbet to freezer container. Cover tightly with lid and freeze until firm, at least 8 hours or overnight. DO AHEAD: Can be made 3 days ahead. Keep frozen.
Divide sorbet among wine goblets or dessert glasses. Serve immediately.

tasty-seasoned-oyster-crackers.name: Tasty Seasoned Oyster Crackers
instructions: Preheat oven to 135°C (135 degrees C).

In a large bowl, mix the canola oil, ranch dressing mix, dill, lemon pepper, granulated garlic, onion powder, seasoned salt, and white pepper until thoroughly combined. Gently mix the crackers with the seasonings, tossing to coat the crackers with the seasoned oil. Spread the crackers out onto a jelly roll pan in a single layer.

Bake in the preheated oven until the crackers are very lightly browned at the edges, 15 to 20 minutes, stirring to bake other sides halfway through cooking. Allow to cool completely before storing in airtight canister or resealable plastic bag.

the-blt-oyster-stuffing.name: The Blt Oyster Stuffing

ingredients.prep: cut into pieces, divided, plus more
torn into 1-inch pieces (about 10 cups), dried out overnight
white and light green parts only, chopped

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Butter a shallow 3-qt. baking dish and a sheet of foil. Place bread in a very large bowl.

Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add bacon and cook, stirring occasionally and breaking into small pieces with a spoon, until browned and cooked through, 7–10 minutes. Transfer to bowl with bread with a slotted spoon.
Add onions, celery, leek, and thyme to skillet, season with salt and pepper, and cook, stirring often, until onions are golden brown and soft, 10–12 minutes. Transfer to bowl with bread.

Reduce heat to medium and cook Sherry in skillet, scraping up any browned bits, until almost all evaporated, about 1 minute. Add ½ cup butter; cook, stirring, until melted. Drizzle over bread mixture.

Whisk eggs and 2 cups stock in a medium bowl; pour over bread mixture. Season with salt and pepper, add oysters, and toss, adding more stock ¼-cupful at a time as needed (you may not use it all), until combined and bread is hydrated. Transfer to prepared baking dish and dot with remaining ¼ cup butter.

Cover with buttered foil; bake until a paring knife inserted into the center comes out hot, 30–35 minutes. Increase oven temperature to 450°. Uncover and bake until top is golden brown and crisp, 20–25 minutes. Let sit 10 minutes before serving.
Do Ahead: Stuffing can be assembled 1 day ahead. Cover and chill.

the-lungarno.name: The Lungarno
instructions: For the froth: Add the lemons, sugar and 3/4 cup water to a blender. Blend until smooth, then strain the mixture through a fine mesh strainer.

For the drink: Add ice to an all-purpose wine glass. Pour in the amaro and 1 1/2 ounces lemon froth and stir to combine (save the remaining lemon froth for another use). Top with the Champagne and garnish with the lemon twist.

thousand-island-dressing.name: Thousand Island Dressing
instructions: Combine mayonnaise, ketchup, pickle relish, Worcestershire sauce, horseradish, and paprika in a bowl; mix well. Cover with plastic wrap and chill before serving.

thyme-chicken-saute-recipe-lezzet.name: Thyme Chicken Saute Recipe

ingredients.item: 2 tablespoon olive oil or liquid oil

instructions: 1. Eat chickens directly and salt, light with pepper & spice it.
2. Heat the olive oil in a large pan, add onions and yellowms and roast.
3. Take chicken piece pan and soak the color in medium fire to white.
4. Add peppers and add a few roast with minutes.
5. Add and mix butter then add hot water.
6. Add thyme and pepper, close the cover of the ceiling and cook for 10-15 minutes in jealous fire.
7. Get from the stove when chickens get softened and sos consistency and serve hot.
8.  Afiyet olsun.

tiramisu-ice-cream-argiro.name: Tiramisu Ice Cream

ingredients.item: 1 cup of strong espresso coffee in hot room
1 cup of strong espresso coffee in hot room

instructions: We'll use a little long cake form. We dress her up in a tiramisu ice cream membrane.
Freeze it in the freezer.
Let the coffee cool well.
In bowls, add the yolks with half the sugar and beat with a hand mixer to white, fluffy and double in volume.
We whisk the mascarpone with the marsala wine and pour it into the yolks.
Beat the cream in thick form and add to the mixture.
Beat the whites with salt to form soft peaks at moderate speed.
[adrotate banner=”29′′]
Add the sugar and beat hard for 1 minute, until the meringue is polished.
Add it to the mixture and gently mix with spatula to homogenize.
We're making a layer of cream in the form.
We dip the cookies in the coffee and make a layer of savayar cookies.
Repeat layer of cream – layer of cookies and finish with cream.
Straighten the surface and freeze the ice cream for at least 3 hours until it thickens well.
We're flipping the uniform.
Remove the membrane.
Allow some room temperature to soften.
Cut the tiramisou ice cream into slices and sprinkle cocoa and chocolate rolls.

tiramisu-with-cinnamon-teleculinaria.name: Tiramisu With Cinnamon
instructions: Distribute toothpicks of la reine, broken in half, by cups or glasses. Brush the sticks well with the coffee.
On the other hand, beat the yolks with sugar and liquor. Then add the mascarpone, slowly and stirring well.
Cover the toothpicks with a layer of the cheese preparation, have over more broken toothpicks and brush again with coffee.
Cover with another layer of the remaining cream and sprinkle with cocoa and cinnamon, previously mixed. Take the tiramisu to the fridge for at least 3 hours.

tirititim-syrian-recipe-argiro.name: Tirititim Syrian Recipe

ingredients.item: 2 fresh onions with their tender leaves
A few pieces of Syrian graviera
2 fresh onions with their tender leaves
A few pieces of Syrian graviera

instructions: For tiritim the omelet from Syros, we choose medium sized tight and tender pumpkins, without many seeds.
Clean and wash the pumpkins, cut them in half and then in slices with a thickness of 1 million.
Heat the non-stick pan and add the olive oil.
Add the zucchini and stir in medium to strong heat.Let them wither for a few minutes and soften.
Add the fresh onions and sauce for 1-2 minutes without color.
Gently beat the eggs in a bowl with the fork.We do not want the yolk to be completely joined with the white.
Add the fennel some salt and pepper.
Lower the heat and pour with the eggs.
Add the graviera.
Mix regularly from the bottom to the center to break the egg and dress each pumpkin around.
Bake for about 4’-5’ and serve the tiritim on a plate, with tomato and bread.

titos-summer-heat.name: Tito's Summer Heat
instructions: Muddle cucumber and 2 slices jalapeño in a shaker tin.
Add Tito's Handmade Vodka, lime juice, agave, and ice.
Shake vigorously and strain into a glass over ice.
Top with sparkling water. Garnish with a slice of lime and jalapeño.

toasted-chile-de-arbol-and-tomatillo-sal.name: Toasted Chile De Árbol And Tomatillo Salsa
instructions: Toast chiles in a medium skillet over medium heat, turning often, until starting to brown lightly in spots (don’t char), about 3 minutes. Transfer to a plate.

Place chiles, tomatillos, garlic, and 2½ cups water in a medium saucepan and season with salt. Cover, bring to a simmer, and cook until tomatillos turn olive green and burst, 5–8 minutes. Remove from heat and let sit (still covered) 5 minutes.

Using a slotted spoon, transfer chiles, tomatillos, and garlic to a blender or food processor; add ½ cup cooking liquid from saucepan and purée until smooth. Add lime juice and season with salt. Let cool before serving.
Do Ahead: Salsa can be made 3 days ahead. Cover and chill.

toasted-pumpkin-seeds.name: Toasted Pumpkin Seeds
instructions: Scrape seeds and any attached fibers from pumpkin using a large metal spoon. Place seeds in a colander and rinse well to help separate seeds from fibers. Set seeds aside. Cook seeds in a medium saucepan of boiling salted water until beginning to look translucent, 5-7 minutes. Drain and transfer to a paper towel-lined baking sheet; pat well to dry.
Preheat oven to 425°. Spread seeds on a rimmed baking sheet, toss with olive oil, and season with salt and pepper. Roast until golden and crisp, 12—15 minutes. Let cool completely.
After seasoning seeds with olive oil, salt, and pepper, toss with one of the following combinations:
1/2 tsp. each turmeric and chile powder
1 tsp. Za’atar and 1/2 tsp. finely grated lemon zest
1/2 tsp. Aleppo pepper, 1/2 tsp. ground cumin
1/2 tsp. ground Szechuan peppercorns
1 Tbsp. sugar and 1/4 tsp. cinnamon

tofu-scramble-breakfast-burrito.name: Tofu Scramble Breakfast Burrito
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200°C).
Add the potatoes to a baking sheet with a drizzle of olive oil, the paprika, oregano, ½ teaspoon of garlic powder, salt, and pepper and toss until well coated.
Bake for 20 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the potatoes are tender.

Heat a drizzle of olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Once the oil begins to shimmer, crumble the block of tofu into the pan until it resembles the consistency of scrambled eggs, being careful not to over-crumble because it will continue to break apart as you cook. Cook for 4-5 minutes, until the tofu is slightly golden.

Add the soy sauce, nutritional yeast, the remaining 2½ teaspoons garlic powder, the onion powder, turmeric, salt, and pepper and stir until well combined. Add the tomatoes and cook for 4-5 minutes, until the tomatoes become slightly wrinkled. Add the spinach and cook until wilted, 3-4 minutes.

To assemble a burrito, add ¼ of the tofu scramble, ¼ of the potatoes, some avocado, and hot sauce to the center of a tortilla. Fold in the sides and roll up, keeping the filling tucked in place. Repeat with the remaining ingredients. Cut in half and serve.
Enjoy!

tomato-and-cheese-pancake-recipe-lezzet.name: Tomato And Cheese Pancake Recipe

ingredients.item: 1.5 cup grated white cheese or cheese Home
By using full wheat flour, you can prepare a chest if you are using the fiber rate heart.
You can get a balanced consistency with a mixture of half white flour + half full wheat flour.
A lighter interior with Lor cheese; mortar you can prepare.
You can get a more spaced and soft texture using Kasar cheese.
You can catch a delicious and balanced taste with white cheese + baker mixture.
You can get more dense aromas using dried tomatoes (min chopped).
I&ccedil adding pepper to the container with tomato; you can enrich the harper.
Instead of butter, you can enjoy a lighter taste using olive oil.
After cooking very little butter suedyou can improve flavor.
You can add fine chopped parsley, dill or fresh fesle.
With a pinch pepper, pepper or thyme, you can taste strengthened.

instructions: 1. Take a deep rough of the flour and salt, kneading a dough that is soft but not addressed by adding warm water.
2. Cut the dough into 4 equal glands, u list to the car coverp 15 minutes.
3. Mix yogurt with small cubeunch in cheese, cheese (and eaten).
4. A&ccedil in order to examine the glands;
5. Internal broadcast in half of the dough.
6. Close the dough and press the edges of seal.
7. In the middle fire in the heated pan, cook both sides turnally.
8. After Haze u with a small amount of butter warm service.
9.  Afiyet olsun.

tomato-frittata.name: Tomato Frittata

ingredients.prep: whites and greens separated, finely chopped

instructions: Place a rack in upper third of oven and preheat broiler. Beat egg whites and yolks in a medium bowl; season with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a large ovenproof cast-iron or nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add spinach and scallion whites and cook until spinach is wilted, about 2 minutes. Pour eggs over spinach mixture, shaking pan to evenly distribute, and cook until edges and bottom are just set, about 30 seconds. Top with scallion greens and dill, then tomatoes and feta. Season lightly with salt and pepper and transfer skillet to oven. Broil until frittata is golden brown and center is set, about 4 minutes.

tomato-salad-with-feta.name: Tomato Salad With Feta
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Toast pistachios on a rimmed baking sheet, tossing once, until golden brown, 6–8 minutes. Let cool, then chop.

Arrange tomatoes on a plate; season lightly with salt and pepper. Mix vinaigrette, ¼ cup parsley, and two-thirds of pistachios in a small bowl. Drizzle over tomatoes. Top with feta, parsley leaves, and remaining pistachios.

tomato-spice-cake.name: Tomato Spice Cake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a 9x13-inch cake pan and set aside.
Whisk cake mix, tomato soup, milk, eggs. and cinnamon together in a bowl until well combined. Pour into the prepared pan.

Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, 28 to 33 minutes. Cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes. Run a table knife around the edges to loosen. Invert carefully onto a serving plate or cooling rack. Let cool completely.

tomato-toast-with-chives-and-sesame-seed.name: Tomato Toast With Chives And Sesame Seeds
instructions: Mix garlic and mayonnaise in a small bowl. Squeeze about 1 tsp. juice from lemon half into bowl; season with kosher salt. Hang on to lemon.

Spread a generous layer of seasoned mayonnaise over toast; shingle a few tomato slices on top. Sprinkle with chives, sesame seeds, sea salt, black pepper, and Aleppo-style pepper. Finely grate some lemon zest from reserved lemon over and drizzle with some oil.

top-secret-chocolate-mousse.name: Top-secret Chocolate Mousse
instructions: Place chocolate in medium metal bowl. Set bowl over saucepan of simmering water and stir until chocolate is melted and smooth. Remove bowl from over water. Add egg yolks to melted chocolate and whisk until smooth. Using electric mixer, beat egg whites and pinch of salt in another medium bowl until soft peaks form. Gradually add 1 1/2 teaspoons sugar, beating constantly until whites are glossy and medium-firm peaks form. Using silicone or rubber spatula, fold 1/4 of beaten whites into chocolate mixture to lighten. Gently fold remaining whites into chocolate mixture just until incorporated (do not overmix or mixture may deflate).
Divide mousse among 4 bowls. Cover and chill until set, about 4 hours. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.
Beat cream and 1 teaspoon sugar in another medium bowl until peaks form. Spoon whipped cream atop mousse.

traditional-chicken-milanese-in-the-air-.name: Traditional Chicken Milanese, In The Air Fryer, Or Baked
traditional-chicken-milanese-in-the-air-.original_name: Milanesa de pollo tradicional, en freidora de aire, o al horno

ingredients.item: Optional: a lemon chorrit and some grated cheese for bouncing

instructions: He cuts the 2 chicken breasts into steaks about half an inch. If something bad remains, you can hit with a deck or with the base of a glass and put them up. That's how they'll cook evenly and be more tender.
In a big bowl, beat up 3 eggs with some salt, pepper and 2 very fine minced garlic teeth. Also add a good handful of chopped fresh parsley and, if you like, a lemon chorrit.
Put the chicken steaks inside and move well so they get completely covered. Let them rest at least 15 or 20 minutes, but if you have time, an hour in the refrigerator greatly improves the result.

Meanwhile, prepare the padded bread (about 200 grams). If you mix it with some grated cheese, even better, because it's more tasty. Pass every stick from the adobo to the frosted bread and press with his hands to stick well.
If you want them to get more crispy, you can repeat the passage: egg again and bread pitted again.

Warm abundant soft olive oil in a large frying pan. When the oil's hot (about 170-3) and if you have thermometer, but if you don't, just bubble with a few pieces of bread), fry the milanese. About two or three minutes per side are usually sufficient, until they are golden and crispy.
Take them and place them on paper and cook them so that they run out their excess oil.

traditional-childrens-recipe-teleculinar.name: Traditional Children's Recipe
instructions: Light a pot with water and salt, add the pumpkin and let it cook. Once cooked, drain it well, crush it and let it love. Add the milk, lemon shaving, yeast, eggs and flour and knead very well. Tape with a damp cloth and let it leave till it is double the volume.
Then remove pieces of dough and stretch it with your fingertips until it is very thin.
Bring a frying pan with oil to the fire when it's hot enough to fry your children. Then remove and let it run down on absorbent paper. Pass them through yellow sugar and serve decorated to taste.

traditional-sesame-halva-comedera.name: Traditional Sesame Halva

ingredients.item: 250 g de tahini (mejor claro y fluido)
80 g de pistachos pelados y troceados
Woven paper or small rectangular mold

instructions: He slightly holds his pistachios in a dry frying pan, only till they shed smell. Let them cool and save them.

Put the sugar and water into a den over medium heat and let it simmer without stirring. Cooking up to a light syrup, about 115-118 ″ 3 ″ if you use thermometer, or till you form a thin yarn by falling from the spoon.
While making the syrup, mix the tahi with salt and vanilla into a large bowl. Remove to homogenize and leave ready by fire.
Fold the hot syrup onto the taheni at once. A blend with a firm and fast spatula: at first it appears to separate, but in seconds it begins to weigh and become matt.
Add pistachios and mix them just to distribute them. Don't get your jobs worked up: if you continue to move, the halva will get rough and dry.
Pasa la mezcla al molde forrado con papel de horno y presiona bien para compactar. Alisa la superficie sin aplastar en exceso.
Deja enfriar completamente a temperatura ambiente y refrigera al menos 2 horas antes de cortar.

traditional-soft-pretzels.name: Traditional Soft Pretzels

ingredients.item: Barley malt syrup or 1 tbsp firmly packed dark brown sugar

instructions: Sprinkle the yeast over the warm water in the bowl of a stand mixer or in a large bowl. Add the barley malt syrup, stirring until it is dissolved. Allow the yeast to bloom until it is foamy, 5 to 7 minutes. Stir in the flour, beer, butter, and salt and continue stirring to form a shaggy mass. Attach the bowl and the dough hook to the stand mixer and begin kneading on medium-low speed. After about 1 minute the dough will form a smooth ball. The dough should be quite firm and may be slightly tacky, but not sticky. (If it is sticky, add a little more flour, about 1 tbsp at a time, and knead it in until the dough is smooth. If the dough is too dry to come together, add more water, 1 tsp at a time.) Continue kneading the dough on medium-low speed until it is elastic, 5 to 7 minutes. Alternatively, turn the shaggy dough out onto an unfloured work surface and knead it by hand.

Choose a bowl that will be large enough to contain the dough after it has doubled in size, and grease it lightly with butter. Transfer the dough to the greased bowl and cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap. Put the dough in the refrigerator to rise for at least 8 hours, and up to 24 hours, for optimal flavor.
For quick pretzels, allow the dough to rise at room temperature (in a warm spot) until it has doubled in size, about 1 1/2 hours.
Line two 12-by-17-in/30.5-by-43-cm rimmed baking sheets with parchment paper; set aside.

Turn the dough out onto an unfloured work surface and firmly press it down to deflate. To form the classic pretzel shape, cut the dough into eight equal portions. Work with one piece of dough at a time and keep the rest covered with a damp, clean kitchen towel. Pat a piece of dough down with your fingertips to form a rough rectangle about 3 1/2 by 5 1/2 in/9 by 14 cm. Beginning on a long side, roll the dough up tightly, forming it into a little loaf. Pinch the seam together. Shape the dough into a rope by rolling it against the work surface with your palms and applying mild pressure, working from the center of the dough out to the ends. If you need more friction, spray the counter with a little water from a squirt bottle or drizzle a few drops of water and spread it with your hand. Once you can feel that the dough rope doesn’t want to stretch any farther (usually when it is between 12 to 16 in/30.5 to 40.5 cm long), set it aside to rest and begin shaping another piece in the same manner. Repeat this process with the remaining pieces of dough.

Return to the first dough rope and continue rolling it out to a length of 24 to 28 in/61 to 71 cm, leaving the center about 1 in/2.5 cm in diameter and tapering the ends thinly by applying a little more pressure as you work your way out. Position the dough rope into a U shape, with the ends pointing away from you. Holding an end in each hand, cross the ends about 3 in/7.5 cm from the tips and then cross them again. Fold the ends down and press them into the U at about 4 and 8 o’clock, allowing about 1/4 in/6 mm of the ends to overhang. Place the pretzel on one of the prepared baking sheets and cover it with a damp towel. Repeat this process with the remaining dough, spacing out the pretzels on the baking sheets at least 1 in/2.5 cm apart and covering them with a damp towel.

Allow the covered pretzels to rise at warm room temperature until they have increased in size by about half, 20 to 30 minutes. (The pretzels can be refrigerated at this point, covered tightly with plastic wrap, for up to 8 hours before dipping and baking them.)
At least 20 minutes before baking, position one rack in the upper third and another rack in the lower third of the oven and preheat it to 260°C/260°C/gas 10.
Prepare the lye solution or baked baking soda solution.

Use a large skimmer to gently dip the pretzels in the lye or baked baking soda solution, one or two at a time. Leave them in the solution for about 20 seconds, carefully turning once after 10 seconds. Remove the pretzels from the liquid, drain, and return them to the baking sheets, spacing them at least 1 in/2.5 cm apart. If the ends come detached, simply reposition them. Repeat with the remaining pretzels.

Use a sharp paring knife or razor blade to cut a slit about 1/4 in/6 mm deep in the thickest part of each pretzel (the bottom of the U). Top them as you choose, if desired. Bake the pretzels until they are deep mahogany in color, 8 to 12 minutes, rotating the pans from front to back and top to bottom halfway through the baking time. Transfer the pretzels to a rack to cool for 10 minutes before serving. The pretzels are best enjoyed the day they are made, ideally warm from the oven or within an hour of being baked.

To store the pretzels, allow them to cool completely and then wrap each one individually in plastic wrap. Store them at room temperature for up to 2 days, or put the plastic-wrapped pretzels in a resealable plastic freezer bag and freeze them for up to 1 month. Reheat the pretzels in a 177°C/180°C/gas 4 oven for about 5 minutes, or for 10 to 12 minutes if frozen.

tres-leches-cake-traditional-recipe-come.name: Tres Leches Cake, Traditional Recipe

ingredients.item: 1 heccup of wheat flour every use
1/2 taza de mantequilla sin sal, fundida y enfriada ((1 barra))

instructions: It purifies the oven to 180 been 3 been (177 been 3). Fold a rectangular mold with butter and sprinkle slightly with flour. Reserve.
En un bol hondo, mezcla la leche condensada, leche evaporada y la media crema hasta obtener una mezcla uniforme. Reserva en el refrigerador.
En otro recipiente, mezcla la harina, el polvo de hornear y la sal. Revuelve con varilla manual para airear.

In a big bowl, beat your eggs one by one for a minute with an electric mixer. Add the sugar slowly till the mixture is thick and light yellow. He then adds his vanilla and his butter as a yarn and keeps beating.
It incorporates the dry ingredients into the egg mix, in parts, with wrapped movements using a spatula. Not about mixes to maintain airy texture.
Vierte la mezcla en el molde y hornea durante 30-35 minutos.
You can also put a stick into the centre of the biscuit till it's clear. That's how you get her out of the oven.

Once out of the oven, let the cake cool 10 minutes. Fold with a fork across the surface and slowly pour the 3-milks mixture. It refrigerates at least 4 hours (ideally throughout the night) to well absorb the liquid.
Bat the cream to beat with sugar and vanilla till you get firm peaks. Cover the cake before serving.

trinxat-de-la-cerdanya-traditional-catal.name: Trinxat De La Cerdanya (traditional Catalan Recipe)
trinxat-de-la-cerdanya-traditional-catal.original_name: Trinxat de la Cerdanya (receta tradicional catalana)

ingredients.item: 3-4 cucharadas de aceite de oliva virgen extra

instructions: It starts by peeling and shredding potatoes and cabbage into small pieces. In a large pan, boils water with salt and adds the potatoes. Cooking the potatoes till they're half baked, which will take about 10-12 minutes. He then adds his cabbage to his casserole and keeps boiling up till both his potatoes and his cabbage are well cooked and tender and will take another 10-12 minutes about. Once cooked, scattering well the potatoes and cabbage and place them in a sharp fountain. With the help of a fork, start with "trinxar" or pounding potatoes and cabbage into a uniform purée. Put the mixture aside.

While cabbage and potatoes are resting, heat up a big frying pan with medium heat. In a frying pan, heats some extra virgin olive oil and then pits and laminates his garlic teeth. Fry them in the oil till they are golden and fragrant, and then remove them from the frying pan and preserve them. At the same frying pan, add the pancet cut into pieces and fry till crisp.

Remove the pancet from the frying pan and with the remaining oil in the frying pan, heat up the mixture of cabbage and potato with medium-high heat and cook it from both sides till it's golden and crispy. That'll take about 5- 7 minutes from each end. Once the Trinxat is golden and crispy, put it into a serving source.
Finally, scatter the fried garlic and crispy pantet above the Trinxat and serve it immediately.

true-amaretti.name: True Amaretti
instructions: Beat egg whites with an electric mixer fitted with the whisk attachment until foamy. Gradually add sugar and almond extract, continuing to beat until very stiff peaks form. Switch to the paddle attachment and mix in almond meal until dough comes together.
Cover cookie dough with plastic wrap and refrigerate, 3 hours to overnight.
Preheat oven to 163°C (165 degrees C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
Pour confectioners' sugar into a shallow bowl. Roll cookie dough into small balls and coat with confectioners' sugar. Arrange on the baking sheets.
Bake cookies in the preheated oven until lightly browned and firm, 10 to 15 minutes.

tuk-trey-ping-poh-with-pork-chops.name: Tuk Trey Ping Poh With Pork Chops
instructions: Heat broiler. Spread out tomatoes, shallots, garlic, and chiles on a rimmed baking sheet; broil, turning halfway through, until tomatoes are charred and beginning to burst, about 10 minutes. Let cool slightly.
Meanwhile, heat a large cast-iron skillet over high. Season pork chops generously with salt and pepper; rub with oil. Cook pork until browned, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer to a platter.

Transfer charred vegetables to a food processor; pulse to a coarse paste (you want some texture). Transfer sauce to a bowl and stir in lime juice, cilantro, fish sauce, and sugar. Taste and season with more fish sauce if needed.
Serve pork chops with rice and sauce for dipping.

turkey-club-with-fried-stuffing.name: Turkey Club With Fried Stuffing
instructions: Cook bacon in a small skillet until brown and crisp; remove.
Pour off most of the fat in skillet; cook stuffing (packing together if needed) until browned on both sides and warmed through.
Build sandwich with marble rye, mayonnaise, bacon, baby arugula, turkey, fried stuffing (standing in as middle slice of bread), and tomato slices.

turkey-cordon-bleu-with-mashed-potatoes-.name: Turkey Cordon Bleu With Mashed Potatoes
turkey-cordon-bleu-with-mashed-potatoes-.original_name: Cordon bleu de peru com puré de batata
instructions: Start by preparing the mashed potatoes: peel and cut the potatoes in rooms. Cook them in boiling water, seasoned with salt. Brush them, reduce them to puree and add the milk to the desired thickness. Then add the butter, salt, pepper, nutmeg and chopped parsley, stir well, remove from the fire and reserve.
Season all the steaks with salt and pepper. Extend four of them on a board and put a slice of cheese on top. Sprinkle with thyme, have a slice of ham and then another one of cheese.
Cover the cheese slices with the remaining seasoned steaks, close with a toothpick and take it to the fridge for a while.
Then pass the steaks for flour, egg beaten and bread grated and fry in very hot oil until they are golden. Drain well on absorbent paper and serve with mashed potatoes.

twice-baked-loaded-potatoes.name: Twice-baked Loaded Potatoes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400˚F (200˚C).
Rub each potato with 1 teaspoon of oil, and prick the top with a fork.
Place the potatoes on a rimmed baking sheet and bake for 1 hour.
Transfer potatoes to a cutting board, and cut in half.
Using a spoon, gently scoop out the inside flesh of the potatoes and transfer to a medium bowl. Reserve the skins.
Add the sour cream, melted butter, salt, pepper, ½ of the shredded cheese, and ½ of the scallions, and combine with a hand mixer until smooth.
Use a spoon to transfer the potato mixture back into the scooped out potato skins.
Bake for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle the potatoes with remaining cheese, and bake for 5 more minutes, until the cheese is melted.
Top with sour cream, bacon, and the remaining scallions.
Enjoy!

twice-baked-sweet-potatoes-with-bacon-se.name: Twice-baked Sweet Potatoes With Bacon-sesame Brittle
instructions: Line a rimmed baking sheet with a silicone baking mat or parchment paper.
Cook bacon in a medium nonstick skillet over medium heat until most of the fat is rendered and bacon is starting to crisp. Transfer bacon to a sieve set over a small bowl; reserve drippings.

Return bacon, 1 Tbsp. drippings, sugar, and sesame seeds to same skillet. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, until sugar turns the color of milk chocolate, about 5 minutes. Transfer mixture to prepared baking sheet and use a spatula to spread out evenly; let cool. Break brittle into shards. DO AHEAD: Can be made 1 day ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.
Preheat oven to 400°. Place sweet potatoes on a foil-lined baking sheet. Roast until tender, 45–55 minutes. Let sit until cool enough to handle.

Slice potatoes in half lengthwise. Working over a large bowl, scoop out flesh from 8 halves, leaving a 1/2-inch-thick layer inside skins. Place potato halves on same foil-lined baking sheet. Scoop flesh from remaining 4 halves; discard skins. Mash flesh with a whisk; add eggs, butter, miso, and ginger and stir until mixture is smooth. Spoon or pipe filling into reserved skins. DO AHEAD: Can be made 6 hours ahead. Cover and chill.
Bake potatoes until the tops are lightly puffed and golden brown, 30–35 minutes (potatoes will take longer if they've been chilled). Top potatoes with bacon-sesame brittle and scallions.
